title: Bug in VVP grammar? .port_info statements don't end in a semicolon.
body:
I discovered this while writing my own toy parser for vvp files.

Every single statement must end with a semicolon. with the small exception of `.port_info` statements, which a defined in `vvp/parse.y` (line 771) as not ending in a semicolon. I'm guessing this was an issue with the initial implementation of the port info feature. I've checked and there are no other exceptions.

Likewise, tgt-vvp skips the semicolon when outputting `.port_info` statements. 

It looks like it's reasonably easy to fix, but there is a question of backwards/forwards compatibility. My quick testing shows that adding a semicolon to the end of the `.port_info` statement will parse in current versions of vvp (I suspect it's using the empty statement rule). This means we can just update tgt-vvp to include the semicolon.    
But if we update the grammar in `vvp/parse.y` to enforce semicolons then old vvp scripts will no-longer work in newer versions. It might be a good idea to make the semicolon optional, and maybe print a warning message. Or we can just add a comment to `vvp/parse.y` explaining why it's missing for future reference.  

I'm more than happy to submit a pull request. I just wanted to consult on the correct way of dealing with backwards compatibility. 

Thanks.
